dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9309 IH: Promoting Responsive Inclusion and Diverse Engagement Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Williams of Georgia (for herself, Mr. Takano, Ms. Tlaib, Ms. Titus, Mr. Espaillat, Mr. Johnson of Georgia, Ms. Norton, Mr. García of Illinois, and Mr. Krishnamoorthi) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

form.official-title: To amend the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to expand the Offices of Minority and Women Inclusion to encompass LGBTQI+ inclusion, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Promoting Responsive Inclusion and Diverse Engagement Act of 2026 or the PRIDE Act of 2026.

section.header: Expanding the Offices of Minority and Women Inclusion to encompass LGBTQI+ inclusion

subsection.text: Section 342 of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act (12 U.S.C. 5452) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading, by striking AND WOMEN and inserting , WOMEN, AND LGBTQI+;
by striking Office of Minority and Women Inclusion each place such term appears in heading or text and inserting Office of Minority, Women, and LGBTQI+ Inclusion;
by striking and women-owned each place such term appears and inserting , women-owned, and LGBTQI+-owned;
in subsection (b)(2)(A), by striking and gender and inserting gender, gender identity, and sexual orientation;

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1), by inserting LGBTQI+ individuals, after women,;
in paragraph (2), by striking and minorities and inserting , minorities, and LGBTQI+ individuals; and
in paragraph (3)(A), by striking and women and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+ individuals;
in paragraph (3), by striking and women and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+; and
in paragraph (4), by striking and women and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+;

paragraph.text: in subsection (f), by striking and women each place such term appears and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+ individuals; and
in subsection (g), by adding at the end the following:
The term LGBTQI+ means lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, or intersex.
The term LGBTQI+-owned business means a business—

subparagraph.text: more than 50 percent of the ownership or control of which is held by 1 or more LGBTQI+ individuals; and
more than 50 percent of the net profit or loss of which accrues to 1 or more LGBTQI+ individuals.

paragraph.header: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
paragraph.text: The Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act (12 U.S.C. 5301 et seq.) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the table of contents in section 1(b), in the item relating to section 342, by striking AND WOMEN and inserting , WOMEN, AND LGBTQI+; and
in section 1016(c)(9), by striking Office of Minority and Women Inclusion and inserting Office of Minority, Women, and LGBTQI+ Inclusion.

paragraph.header: Housing and Community Development Act of 1992
paragraph.text: Section 1319A of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 (12 U.S.C. 4520) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking Office of Minority and Women Inclusion each place such term appears in heading or text and inserting Office of Minority, Women, and LGBTQI+ Inclusion;
in subsection (b), by striking and women, and minority- and women-owned and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+ individuals, and minority-, women-, and LGBTQI+-owned;

in subsection (e), by striking and women and businesses owned by minorities and women and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+ individuals and businesses owned by minorities, women, and LGBTQI+ individuals; and

clause.text: in paragraph (2), by striking and people of color and inserting , people of color, and LGBTQI+ individuals; and
in paragraph (3), by striking and women to place talented young minorities and women and inserting , women, and LGBTQI+ individuals to place talented young minorities, women, and LGBTQI+ individuals.

subsection.text: Effective on the date of enactment of the Federal Housing Finance Regulatory Reform Act of 2008, section 1161(a) of such Act is amended by striking paragraph (2).
